behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies because correctness depends on real AI/ML transcription diarization output (speaker labeling quality), which stubs would hide. REAL-INFRA also exists (real API), but Agentic is the tighter failure boundary here. Rejected Workflow: behavior text checks one model-stage outcome (label preservation), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels that can be mapped to known attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS indicates this is primarily parsing/structuring logic and can fail within one function boundary without real I/O. No REAL-INFRA or LLM-DEP is required for this behavior wording. Rejected Integration: behavior does not require proving cross-module runtime interaction beyond deterministic parsing output.
behaviors.test_description: Feed fixed transcript snippets with explicit assignments into the parser function and assert exact structured extraction of assignee and task text, including edge formatting cases.

behaviors.description: Live summary includes at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output content coverage (whether the summarizer actually emits action items per assigned attendee). A stubbed LLM would mask this failure (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one summarization decision stage, not a required multi-agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts containing explicit per-attendee task assignments to the real summarization model and verify output has at least one action item mapped to each assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` schema section, not narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a module-boundary handoff/routing check (identified decisions -> structured schema placement), so Integration is the minimum realistic failure boundary. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no dependence on AI output quality if identified decisions are fixture-driven, no multi-agentic chain. Rejected Agentic: behavior text says 'identified decisions', so model judgment quality is out of scope here.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor output fixtures into the schema assembly path and assert decision items land only in `decisions` fields, with no leakage into generic narrative containers.

behaviors.description: Key decisions from transcript appear in summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality validation of real model behavior (decision salience + placement), so LLM-DEP applies directly. Deterministic mocks would hide failures where model buries decisions in narrative. Rejected Workflow: behavior does not explicitly require validating multiple sequential model decisions; one summarization output can expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with clearly stated decisions to the real summarizer and assert those decisions are represented in the `decisions` section rather than only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: For audio >60 minutes, service segments, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP applies: multiple sequential segment-processing stages with model-influenced outputs are merged, and correctness depends on cross-step handoff and final coherence. LLM-DEP is present in segment summaries/merge quality. Rejected System-only: real infra alone is insufficient; core failure can be in multi-agentic sequencing/merge behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through production segmentation + transcription + summarization flow; verify ordered segment processing and that merged structured output remains coherent across segment boundaries (no dropped/duplicated action items or decisions).

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) runs on a real recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly spans the full pipeline with multiple dependent stages including AI model calls, so MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP make Workflow the minimum boundary. Rejected System: System would miss failures caused by stage-to-stage agentic decision interactions across the whole flow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full production path on a real meeting recording from upload through final structured delivery and assert successful autonomous completion plus valid structured sections (action items, decisions, attendee attributions).
